text: Status: Accepted • Author: Jordan Patel • Reviewers: Riley Park, Devon Liu • Target: May 15, 2026

text.t: We're replacing the current auth stack with a session-token model backed by signed cookies and a centralized session store. This eliminates three classes of bugs and unblocks SSO for enterprise customers.
v2 was built when we had one product surface. We now have four, and the auth code has grown to
of overlapping logic. The bug rate has been climbing for two quarters.

text: Rollback plan: feature flag flip is instant. Session store has dual-write enabled until May 22.

items: OAuth proxy (rejected: too much latency for our SPA)
Third-party (Auth0, Clerk) — rejected: cost & data residency

text: Should we issue refresh tokens, or just re-issue on each request when within 24h of expiry? Leaning toward the latter.
